mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, labor market slack signals severe economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains above the threshold for more than 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved revenue expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series signals, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of cases where both RSAFS and HOUST rise month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which translates to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of observations during expansion regimes, or the mechanism reverses direction when inflation exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal strengthening labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict 5-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll revisions consistently precede negative market moves.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 215.0, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL surpasses 215.0, or inflation spikes fail to correlate with subsequent equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based aggregate demand strength, which reduces recession risk and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during late-cycle phases signal capacity utilization exhaustion and impending supply bottlenecks, which compress profit margins and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes during late-cycle regimes are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal loses significance when capacity utilization remains above 80%.
